title: Which releases have what translations
wikitext:
[]: Which releases have what translations?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Tue Oct 29, 2024 9:39 am Been pretty out of the loop with Eva stuff for the past few ears but I'm a bit confused about the differences between subtitle tracks on various releases of Evangelion and the Rebuild movies i.e Netflix and the old Platinum sets. From what I gather the newer subtitle tracks for the Netflix release have a more direct/literal translation but I'm not sure if these are on the newer blu rays or not.

[]: Re: Which releases have what translations?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Oct 29, 2024 4:35 pm I'd recommend you go with the Netflix translation because it is the first one made directly by the show's actual creators. Old ones were made in the 90s and early 00s and are full of mistakes. We in this very forum have had to deal with those for 20 years before Netflix came along. Now, IIRC the G-KIDS release only has Netflix as well but includes the older ones in the more expensive options. People complaining about them being "literal" are mostly diehard fans of the older localizations that can't get over them. This is, like I said elsewhere, a trait mostly unique to English-speaking fandom who is very protective of their localizations "improving" the source material, in this case even including extra sound effects and stuff. This wiki article was partly born out of one controversy around it, but the Eva English localizations were the source of much, much more fan drama over the years, which are also partly covered. This 2016 video is a good example, and back then people would very aggressively recoil at people criticizing a translation they grew up with.

[]: Which releases have what translations?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Tue Oct 29, 2024 9:39 am Been pretty out of the loop with Eva stuff for the past few ears but I'm a bit confused about the differences between subtitle tracks on various releases of Evangelion and the Rebuild movies i.e Netflix and the old Platinum sets. From what I gather the newer subtitle tracks for the Netflix release have a more direct/literal translation but I'm not sure if these are on the newer blu rays or not.

[]: Re: Which releases have what translations?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Oct 29, 2024 4:35 pm I'd recommend you go with the Netflix translation because it is the first one made directly by the show's actual creators. Old ones were made in the 90s and early 00s and are full of mistakes. We in this very forum have had to deal with those for 20 years before Netflix came along. Now, IIRC the G-KIDS release only has Netflix as well but includes the older ones in the more expensive options. People complaining about them being "literal" are mostly diehard fans of the older localizations that can't get over them. This is, like I said elsewhere, a trait mostly unique to English-speaking fandom who is very protective of their localizations "improving" the source material, in this case even including extra sound effects and stuff. This wiki article was partly born out of one controversy around it, but the Eva English localizations were the source of much, much more fan drama over the years, which are also partly covered. This 2016 video is a good example, and back then people would very aggressively recoil at people criticizing a translation they grew up with.
